indicator_text: Index of lobby regulation (composite of all 22 items)
scoring_rule: The sum of all 22 items indicated the state's index score.
source_quote: The sum of all 22 items indicated the state's index score. [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:151]
notes: Composite. Decomposes into the 3 sub-category sums (def.section_total, disclosure.section_total, enforce.section_total) which themselves decompose into the 22 atomic items below. Range 0-22. Table 1 caption shows observed range 0-18 across 47 states ("0 - weakest / 18 - strongest"). Data unavailable for Montana, South Dakota, Virginia.

indicator_text: Statutory definition of a lobbyist (7 items)
scoring_rule: Sum of seven 1/0 items below.
source_quote: (1) statutory definition of a lobbyist (seven items) [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:92-93]
notes: Composite (sub-aggregate). Decomposes into the 7 def.* items below. Each sub-item coded 1 if criterion is included in state's definition, 0 if not.

indicator_text: legislative lobbying as a criterion for definition
scoring_rule: each coded 1 if a criterion was included in the state's definition of lobbyists and 0 if it was not
source_quote: Only two states - Arkansas and Illinois which have no statutory designation of lobbyists - fail to include 'legislative' lobbying as a criterion for definition. [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:103-105]
notes: Operational definition is from CSG Blue Book 1988-89 (Campaign Finance, Ethics and Lobby Law: Special Edition); Opheim does not give an explicit textual rule for what counts as "legislative lobbying." BoS-defined.

indicator_text: administrative lobbying as a criterion for definition
scoring_rule: each coded 1 if a criterion was included in the state's definition of lobbyists and 0 if it was not
source_quote: By contrast, thirty states fail to include 'administrative' lobbying as a criterion. [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:105-106]
notes: Operational definition is from CSG Blue Book 1988-89; Opheim does not give an explicit textual rule. BoS-defined.

indicator_text: elective officials designated as lobbyists
scoring_rule: each coded 1 if a criterion was included in the state's definition of lobbyists and 0 if it was not
source_quote: Seven states designate 'elective officials' as lobbyists [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:106-107]
notes: Coded as inclusion criterion for lobbyist definition. BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89).

indicator_text: public employees designated as lobbyists
scoring_rule: each coded 1 if a criterion was included in the state's definition of lobbyists and 0 if it was not
source_quote: while seven others designate 'public employees.' [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:107-108]
notes: Coded as inclusion criterion for lobbyist definition. BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89).

indicator_text: specific compensation standard to delineate lobbying activity
scoring_rule: each coded 1 if a criterion was included in the state's definition of lobbyists and 0 if it was not
source_quote: some states include specific compensation, expenditure, and time standards to delineate lobbying activity. [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:107-109]
notes: Atomization call: Opheim names 3 "standards" in one phrase ("compensation, expenditure, and time"); paper explicitly says the 7 def items include these as separate criteria, so I split them. BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89). Threshold values not specified by Opheim.

indicator_text: specific expenditure standard to delineate lobbying activity
scoring_rule: each coded 1 if a criterion was included in the state's definition of lobbyists and 0 if it was not
source_quote: some states include specific compensation, expenditure, and time standards to delineate lobbying activity. [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:107-109]
notes: Atomization call: separated from compensation/time standards in the same sentence. BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89). Threshold values not specified.

indicator_text: specific time standard to delineate lobbying activity
scoring_rule: each coded 1 if a criterion was included in the state's definition of lobbyists and 0 if it was not
source_quote: some states include specific compensation, expenditure, and time standards to delineate lobbying activity. [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:107-109]
notes: Atomization call: separated from compensation/expenditure standards in the same sentence. BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89). Threshold values not specified.

indicator_text: Frequency and quality of disclosure (8 items)
scoring_rule: Sum of one frequency item plus seven dichotomous information-category items.
source_quote: (2) frequency and quality of disclosure (eight items) [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:93]
notes: Composite (sub-aggregate). Decomposes into 1 frequency item + 7 information-category items below.

indicator_text: Frequency of reporting required of lobbyists
scoring_rule: States which require reports to be filed monthly during the session or both in and out of session were coded 1, while states which require reports quarterly, semi-annually, or annually were coded 0.
source_quote: Frequency of reporting was coded dichotomously to reflect the most extreme differences in frequency. States which require reports to be filed monthly during the session or both in and out of session were coded 1 [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:115-118]
notes: One of two items where finer distinctions were lost in 0/1 coding (per Opheim, line 152-154). Source: CSG Book of the States 1988-89 (the only item NOT from Blue Book per footnote 1).

indicator_text: lobbyist's total spending
scoring_rule: These seven categories were each coded dichotomously depending on whether the state required they be reported.
source_quote: Information disclosed in these reports includes the lobbyist's total spending [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:119;128-129]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89).

indicator_text: spending by category
scoring_rule: These seven categories were each coded dichotomously depending on whether the state required they be reported.
source_quote: spending by category [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:129-130]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89). Opheim does not enumerate the categories.

indicator_text: expenditures benefitting public employees including gifts
scoring_rule: These seven categories were each coded dichotomously depending on whether the state required they be reported.
source_quote: expenditures benefitting public employees including gifts [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:130]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89). "Including gifts" is part of the verbatim phrasing - kept as one item rather than split.

indicator_text: legislation approved or opposed by the lobbyist
scoring_rule: These seven categories were each coded dichotomously depending on whether the state required they be reported.
source_quote: legislation approved or opposed by the lobbyist [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:130-131]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89).

indicator_text: sources of income
scoring_rule: These seven categories were each coded dichotomously depending on whether the state required they be reported.
source_quote: sources of income [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:131]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89).

indicator_text: total income
scoring_rule: These seven categories were each coded dichotomously depending on whether the state required they be reported.
source_quote: total income [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:131-132]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89).

indicator_text: other activities that might constitute influence peddling or conflict of interest
scoring_rule: These seven categories were each coded dichotomously depending on whether the state required they be reported.
source_quote: other activities that might constitute influence peddling or conflict of interest [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:132-133]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89). Catch-all category - operational definition fully implicit; Opheim does not enumerate what activities qualify.

indicator_text: Oversight and enforcement of regulations (7 items)
scoring_rule: Sum of one thoroughness item plus six prosecutorial-authority items.
source_quote: (3) oversight and enforcement of regulations (seven items) [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:94]
notes: Composite (sub-aggregate). Decomposes into 1 thoroughness item + 6 prosecutorial-authority items below. Note: Opheim's prose says "Six items measure the rigor of this authority" (line 145) and lists six items, but the section is described as 7 items total - the 7th is the thoroughness-of-review item.

indicator_text: thoroughness of reviews of lobby reports by oversight agency
scoring_rule: the thoroughness of reviews was coded (1 for review of all reports, 0 for less extensive review)
source_quote: Thus the thoroughness of reviews was coded (1 for review of all reports, 0 for less extensive review). [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:140-142]
notes: One of two items where finer distinctions were lost in 0/1 coding (per Opheim, line 152-154); intermediate possibilities (e.g., random/complaint-based review) collapsed to 0. BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89).

indicator_text: whether the agency may subpoena witnesses
scoring_rule: states were coded 1 if the agency possessed this authority and 0 if it did not
source_quote: whether the agency may subpoena witnesses [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:146-147]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89). Refers to the agency or commission charged with regulating lobbyists.

indicator_text: subpoena records
scoring_rule: states were coded 1 if the agency possessed this authority and 0 if it did not
source_quote: subpoena records [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:147]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89).

indicator_text: conduct administrative hearings
scoring_rule: states were coded 1 if the agency possessed this authority and 0 if it did not
source_quote: conduct administrative hearings [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:147-148]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89).

indicator_text: impose administrative fines
scoring_rule: states were coded 1 if the agency possessed this authority and 0 if it did not
source_quote: impose administrative fines, impose administrative penalties [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:148]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89). Atomization quirk: Opheim lists "impose administrative fines" and "impose administrative penalties" as distinct items in the same sentence; kept as separate per Opheim's enumeration even though they are conceptually overlapping. The distinction is unexplained in the paper.

indicator_text: impose administrative penalties
scoring_rule: states were coded 1 if the agency possessed this authority and 0 if it did not
source_quote: impose administrative fines, impose administrative penalties [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:148]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89). See enforce.impose_administrative_fines note - distinction between fines and penalties is unexplained.

indicator_text: file independent court actions
scoring_rule: states were coded 1 if the agency possessed this authority and 0 if it did not
source_quote: or file independent court actions. [papers/text/Opheim_1991__state_lobby_regulation.txt:148-149]
notes: BoS-defined (CSG Blue Book 1988-89).
